full_suite: go test ./engine/btree/... -race -v -count=1 -> ok, all tests pass (TestDelete* incl. new TestDeleteThreeLevelNoSiblingTypeMismatchDataLoss, TestInsert*, TestLookup, TestNodeSerialization), 21.283s
data_loss_repro_closed: New regression test TestDeleteThreeLevelNoSiblingTypeMismatchDataLoss inserts 40,000 sequential keys (real Insert path, confirmed tree depth >= 2 i.e. root->internal->leaf), deletes a 39,900-key prefix (mirroring verification's own 0..39899 repro that failed at i=15525), then asserts every remaining non-deleted key is still found via Lookup with its correct fileID via assertAllLookupable (not just 'no crash'), plus assertStructuralInvariants and assertNoOrphanedPointers. Passes with the fix.
regression_test_efficacy_check: git stash push -- engine/btree/delete.go (reverting only the production fix, keeping the new test), re-ran -run TestDeleteThreeLevelNoSiblingTypeMismatchDataLoss alone against pre-fix delete.go: FAILS with 'Delete("topic15526/page"): expected found=true, got false' -- confirming the new test actually catches the original bug. git stash pop restored the fix; full suite re-confirmed green afterward.
matrix_coverage: All validation-matrix rows covered: leaf borrow/merge among same-type siblings (TestDeleteLeafMerge, TestDeleteInternalMerge unmodified and passing), root collapse/empty-tree/absent-key (TestDeleteEmptyTree, TestDeleteAbsentKey, TestDeleteSingleLeaf, TestDeleteEmptiesSingleLeafTree unmodified and passing), new type-mismatch data-loss regression (TestDeleteThreeLevelNoSiblingTypeMismatchDataLoss, new, passing).
